steps.title: Identify the opening phrase and main clause structure
steps.content: The opening noun phrase describes a specific person: “One of the few African American global explorers during the turn of the 20th century.” After the comma, the main clause begins and needs a subject. The answer depends on which noun fills that subject position.

steps.title: Apply the matching rule
steps.content:
When an opening describes a person, the main clause's subject must be that person.

Choice A — Matthew Henson made several treks across Greenland between 1891 and 1909. correctly makes Matthew Henson the subject—he is the explorer described in the opening.

Making Greenland or treks the subject illogically attaches the description of a person to a place or object instead.

Choice B — and 1909 were the years between which Matthew Henson made several treks across Greenland. is incoherent. That matches Choice A — Matthew Henson made several treks across Greenland between 1891 and 1909..
